input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS → iOS + Android.

---

## 1. Xcode (iOS requirement)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- **Version discovery:** Apple documents which Xcode ships with which iOS SDK. MAUI release notes list the minimum supported Xcode version.
- After install, run:
  ```
  sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app
  xcodebuild -runFirstLaunch
  ```
- **Verify:** `xcodebuild -version` prints version & build number.

## 2. .NET SDK (includes MAUI workload tooling)

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest **LTS** or **STS** SDK for macOS (Arm64 for Apple Silicon, x64 for Intel).
- **Version discovery:** The [MAUI release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/whats-new/) specify the minimum .NET SDK version per MAUI release.
- **Verify:** `dotnet --version`

## 3. .NET MAUI Workload

```bash
dotnet workload install maui
```

- This pulls in `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, and platform dependencies.
- **Version discovery:** `dotnet workload search maui` lists available versions.
- **Verify:** `dotnet workload list` — should show `maui` as installed.

## 4. Android SDK / JDK (pulled automatically, but verify)

The MAUI workload installs Android SDK components, but you may need to manage them:

- **OpenJDK** — bundled with the Android workload (`microsoft-openjdk`). No separate install needed.
- **Android SDK** — installed to `~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx` (or the path shown by `dotnet android sdk list`).
- To install specific API levels or build tools:
  ```bash
  dotnet android sdk install --package "platforms;android-35"
  ```
- **Verify:**
  ```bash
  dotnet android sdk list          # shows installed SDK components
  java -version                    # confirms JDK is reachable
  ```

## 5. iOS Simulators

```bash
xcodebuild -downloadPlatform iOS
```

Or via **Xcode → Settings → Platforms → +** to add simulator runtimes.

## 6. IDE (pick one)

| IDE | Source |
|---|---|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit + .NET MAUI extension | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |
| **Visual Studio for Mac** | **Discontinued** — use VS Code or Rider |

## 7. End-to-End Verification

```bash
# Create a new project
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for both platforms
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on iOS Simulator
dotnet run -f net9.0-ios

# Run on Android Emulator (create one first via Android SDK)
dotnet run -f net9.0-android
```

Replace `net9.0` with your actual TFM (check your `.csproj`).

## 8. Troubleshooting Checklist

| Symptom | Fix |
|---|---|
| `xcode-select: error` | `sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app` |
| Workload restore fails | `dotnet workload repair` |
| Android license errors | `dotnet android sdk accept-licenses` |
| Missing simulator | Add via Xcode Settings → Platforms |

## Key Links

- **MAUI docs:** https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/
- **Supported platforms matrix:** https://github.com/dotnet/maui/wiki/Release-Versions
- **Known issues per release:** https://github.com/dotnet/maui/wiki

The critical version dependency chain is: **MAUI release → minimum .NET SDK → minimum Xcode**. Always start from the MAUI release notes to pick compatible versions.
